     The Numbers Business Report

Exorcist: The Beginning (2004)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $41,814,863Details
International Box Office $2,142,678
Worldwide Box Office $43,957,541
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Father Lankester Merrin lost his faith after the atrocities he experienced during World War II. In an attempt to escape his past he travels around the world and is approached to assist by the excavation of a Christian Byzantine church, found in pristine condition in a remote region of Kenya. Only with the church also an ancient evil is unearthed that forces Merrin to face atrocities once again.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$18,054,001 (43.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.32 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:95.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$78,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,803 opening theaters/2,813 max. theaters, 4.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $80,532,325

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 20th, 2004 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: October 29th, 2004 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: March 1st, 2005 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for strong violence and gore, disturbing images and rituals, and for language including some sexual dialogue
Running Time: 114 minutes
Franchise: Exorcist
Cast, crew, or production detail: Legacy Sequel, Prequel
Plot point: Demons, Possessed
Social setting: Supernatural
Subgenre: Supernatural Horror
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Morgan Creek Productions
Production Countries: Morocco, Italy
Languages: English

Only with the church also an ancient evil is unearthed that forces Merrin to face atrocities once again.
